data.title: test-gate: local pre-commit gates pass but CI fails -> 22 test failures and shellcheck warnings not caught locally
data.description:
## Incident Overview
The most recent CI run (24319865314, 2026-04-13) failed with 22 test failures across 3 jobs (Hook Tests: 8 fail, Script Tests: 7 fail, ShellCheck: 2 warnings). All commits that introduced these failures passed the local pre-commit test gate and review gate before being pushed.

## Expected Behavior
The local test gate (pre-commit-test-gate.sh) should catch failures that CI will detect, preventing broken code from reaching main.

## Actual Behavior
The local gate passed, but CI found failures in tests that were not associated with the changed files in .test-index, or tests that are only run in CI (shellcheck, portability checks).

## Root Cause Hypothesis
1. .test-index does not map all source files to all tests that validate them — portability tests, reference tests, and shellcheck are not covered
2. The local test gate only runs tests associated with staged files via .test-index; broad suite tests (shellcheck, no-plugin-markdown-refs, no-relative-paths) are CI-only
3. Some failures were introduced by changes in this session (e.g., harvest-worktree.sh relative paths from f518-bba6 fix)

## Example CI Failures
- harvest-worktree.sh uses SCRIPT_DIR/../hooks relative path (caught by test-plugin-scripts-no-relative-paths.sh, not by local gate)
- REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md contains plugins/dso fallback path (caught by test-no-plugin-markdown-refs.sh, not by local gate)
- shellcheck SC2140/SC2254 warnings in validate-issues.sh and pre-commit-review-gate.sh (shellcheck not in local pre-commit)
- 3 agent files missing behavioral_testing_standard references (test-agent-standard-reference.sh not triggered by local gate)
